examples.title: Top-Down Design: Word Count from Console
examples.description: Demonstrates decomposing a simple console app into input, processing, and output helpers.
examples.pseudocode:
main:
  text <- readLineFromUser()
  count <- countWords(text)
  printReport(text, count)

readLineFromUser:
  prompt user
  return line

countWords(text):
  split text on whitespace
  return number of tokens

printReport(text, count):
  print summary

examples.title: Reusable Helpers: Password Validation
examples.description: Shows decomposing validation into small, testable checks that combine into a final decision.
examples.pseudocode:
isValidPassword(pw):
  return isLongEnough(pw) AND hasUpper(pw) AND hasDigit(pw)

isLongEnough(pw):
  return length(pw) >= 8

hasUpper(pw):
  for each char c in pw:
    if isUppercase(c): return true
  return false

hasDigit(pw):
  for each char c in pw:
    if isDigit(c): return true
  return false

examples.title: Refactoring Loop Logic: Prime Listing
examples.description: Illustrates extracting helpers from a loop-heavy task to increase readability and testability.
examples.pseudocode:
main:
  n <- readUpperBound()
  for i from 2 to n:
    if isPrime(i):
      print i

readUpperBound:
  read int from user
  return it

isPrime(k):
  if k < 2: return false
  for d from 2 to floor(sqrt(k)):
    if k % d == 0: return false
  return true
